title: Enforce maximum date range on order search to protect database performance
description: The order search endpoint currently allows unbounded date ranges for CollectedDateTime, OrderedDateTime, and ReceiptedDateTime. Queries spanning years degrade database performance. Add a 12-month (365-day) maximum span validation to each date pair in OrderSearchValidator, and cover the new rules with unit tests in OrderSearchValidatorTests.

acceptance_criteria.text: OrderSearchValidator rejects a CollectedDateTimeFrom/To pair whose span exceeds 365 days with a clear validation message.
OrderSearchValidator rejects an OrderedDateTimeFrom/To pair whose span exceeds 365 days with a clear validation message.

check.label: At least 3 occurrences of 365 in OrderSearchValidator (one per date pair)
check.subject: repo

acceptance_criteria.text: OrderSearchValidator rejects a ReceiptedDateTimeFrom/To pair whose span exceeds 365 days with a clear validation message.

check.label: Validation error message mentioning date range limit in OrderSearchValidator
check.subject: repo

acceptance_criteria.text: Date ranges of exactly 365 days are accepted (boundary is inclusive).

check.label: Boundary or 365-day span test present in OrderSearchValidatorTests
check.subject: repo

acceptance_criteria.text: The existing passing tests in OrderSearchValidatorTests are not broken by the new rules.

check.label: Dotnet unit tests pass for OrderSearchValidatorTests
check.subject: repo

acceptance_criteria.text: New unit tests covering >365 days (invalid), exactly 365 days (valid), and <365 days (valid) are added to OrderSearchValidatorTests.

check.label: At least 3 new test methods related to 365-day span in OrderSearchValidatorTests
check.subject: repo
